title: web -- Fetch/Search/Open Cheatsheet (no web read -- URL-as-target banned)
desc: wkappbot web tools for HTTP and browser access. fetch = raw HTTP GET. search = Google via Chrome CDP. open = launch/navigate tab. For reading content from a Chrome tab, use a11y read with a grap -- web read is REMOVED because URL-as-target silently retargets the wrong tab.
steps: FETCH: wkappbot web fetch <url> [--max-chars 5000] -- raw HTTP GET (no JS, no CDP)
SEARCH: wkappbot web search "query" [--limit 10] -- Google via Chrome CDP
OPEN: wkappbot web open <url> -- launch browser or navigate existing tab (tab dedup by URL)
READ (banned): wkappbot web read <url> is REMOVED. URL-as-target silently retargets the wrong tab and steals focus. Use a11y read with a grap that pins a specific Chrome window instead
Correct read flow: a11y find "*Chrome*" --deep to discover the grap, then a11y read "{proc:'chrome',domain:'example.com',hwnd:0x...}" to extract rendered text
search requires Chrome running with CDP port open
fetch is for raw HTTP where no JS is needed (static HTML, API endpoints); anything needing JS rendering belongs to a11y read on an open tab
author: claude
